commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3097f4eea10d1b7d60231936721b1e20010e7289)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Anthropic OAuth loopback host validation, derives the redirect URI from the resolved host, and adds helper tests for callback host and URI formatting.

PR surface: Source +24, Tests +20. Total +44 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main and v2026.6.10 can bind the callback server to CALLBACK_HOST while sending a hardcoded localhost redirect_uri; I did not run a live Anthropic OAuth flow in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs human/security review and contributor live proof; the remaining blocker is not safe for an automated fix lane because real Anthropic OAuth behavior must be proven.

target.title: fix(anthropic): restrict OAuth callback host to loopback addresses and derive redirect URI
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Anthropic OAuth helper in `src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.ts` accepted any value for `OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST`, including external addresses like `attacker.example.com`. Meanwhile, `REDIRECT_URI` was hardcoded to `http://localhost:53692/callback` regardless of the configured host. This inconsistency created two issues:

1. **Silent OAuth failure**: Setting `OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST` to a non-localhost value (e.g. `192.168.1.100`) causes the callback server to bind on that address, but the `redirect_uri` sent to `claude.ai` is always `localhost`. The browser redirects to `localhost:53692` while the server listens elsewhere — the OAuth flow breaks silently with no clear error.
2. **Authorization code interception**: If an attacker controls `OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST` (e.g. via compromised `.env` or environment injection), they can redirect the callback server to an attacker-controlled host like `attacker.example.com`. Combined with DNS rebinding, the authorization code could be intercepted, hijacking the session token.

## Why This Change Was Made

This follows the same loopback-whitelist pattern already used by the OpenAI OAuth helper (`extensions/openai/openai-chatgpt-oauth-flow.runtime.ts:34-37`), which validates callback hosts and derives `REDIRECT_URI` from the validated host.

## User Impact

- The `OPENCLAW_OAUTH_CALLBACK_HOST` env var now only accepts loopback addresses: `localhost`, `127.0.0.1`, or `::1`. Any other value throws an error.
- `REDIRECT_URI` is derived from the validated callback host rather than being hardcoded to `localhost`.
- IPv6 loopback (`::1`) is properly wrapped in brackets for URL construction (`http://[::1]:53692/callback`).
- Default callback host remains `127.0.0.1` (unchanged behavior).
- Four new regression tests cover: default host, host rejection, IPv6 bracket wrapping, and 127.0.0.1 redirect URI.
- Fixes #96485

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.test.ts` — 8 passed (4 original + 4 new regression tests)
- `pnpm format:repo` — clean
- Autoreview: clean
- CI: pending

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Anthropic OAuth callback host now restricted to loopback addresses only; redirect URI derived from validated host to prevent host/uri mismatch and authorization code interception.

Real environment tested: Linux 4.19.112, Node 22, commit `3097f4eea1` on branch `fix/issue-96485-oauth-callback-host`

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Run the regression tests
pnpm test src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.test.ts

# Verify the code compiles and types are correct
pnpm tsgo:prod src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```console
$ pnpm test src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668000452/codes/OPENSOURCES/openclaw

 ✓ src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.test.ts (8 tests)

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed (8)

$ pnpm tsgo:prod src/llm/utils/oauth/anthropic.ts
# (exits 0, no type errors)
```

Observed result after fix: All 8 tests pass (4 original + 4 new). The new tests confirm: (1) loopback-only whitelist rejects `0.0.0.0`, (2) default host is `127.0.0.1`, (3) `::1` produces `http://[::1]:53692/callback` with brackets, (4) `127.0.0.1` produces `http://127.0.0.1:53692/callback`. Type check passes cleanly.

What was not tested: End-to-end OAuth dance with a real Anthropic provider (requires live credentials). The fix follows the same tested pattern as the OpenAI OAuth helper which already validated loopback hosts identically. No migration needed — the only valid previous configuration was `localhost`/`127.0.0.1`/`::1`; `0.0.0.0` or other external hosts were never secure and will now error immediately.
target.author: xialonglee
